garo: Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?

english: At the market / To the market

garo: Never translate English SVO word order directly into Garo
Rua (⚠ also means 'to pour' as verb)
Books, paper, leaves, flat / thin objects
The rakka (·) is part of the root word, NOT a suffix. Suffixes are clean strings — never prepend · to a suffix. e.g. root=re·ang + suffix=a → re·ang·a
books, paper, leaves, flat thin objects
to do / to work / to make
Add -na suffix to pronoun/noun for indirect object
Damo = common spoken wait; Sengbo also valid
Hai = expression particle for collective action
All three map to same Garo word
Don't see only with your eyes
Na·a nang pen ko on·a mangenma?
Hoe = yes; mangenva = of course / certainly
Distal demonstrative — differs from Ua (that, nearby)
Baitarong = how many; Baita = how much
Suffix: ang·ming = with me; nangming = with you
In the letter — chitthio = letter+location suffix

english: People say it's good, is it good?
In a broad daylight are you sleeping?
No need to thank me, you're welcome

garo: The rakka (·) is a phonetic marker belonging to the root word. Suffixes are always clean strings.
Words with multiple meanings depending on context
